original_hypothesis.title: Inverted Swap Direction in _swapOFTForNote Makes All Swaps Fail
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In _swapOFTForNote, the `isBuy` parameter to CrocSwap is set to `!isNoteBase`. The intent is to sell asdUSDC for NOTE, but the logic is inverted. When NOTE is base token, isBuy=false means 'sell base (NOTE) for quote (asdUSDC)' — the opposite of what's needed. When NOTE is quote, isBuy=true means 'buy base (asdUSDC) with quote (NOTE)' — also wrong. In both cases, the swap attempts to sell NOTE that the router doesn't hold, causing every swap to fail.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This logic error renders the entire cross-chain minting pipeline non-functional. Every user's transaction falls through to the refund path, receiving asdUSDC instead of ASD tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: isBuy=!isNoteBase passed to calcImpact and swap; correct value should be isNoteBase to buy NOTE with asdUSDC

preconditions: Any lzCompose call that reaches the swap step
Ambient pool exists for asdUSDC/NOTE pair

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: !isNoteBase used for isBuy parameter — inverts buy/sell direction
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: When NOTE is base: isBuy=false sells NOTE (router doesn't hold NOTE). When NOTE is quote: isBuy=true buys asdUSDC with NOTE (also wrong direction).
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should use isNoteBase for isBuy (buy NOTE when NOTE is base, sell non-NOTE when NOTE is quote)

analysis_questions: Verify Ambient DEX semantics for isBuy parameter
Does calcImpact with wrong direction return flows that pass the minAmount check?
Does the actual swap.call revert or silently fail with wrong direction?
validation_requirements: Trace swap parameter mapping for both NOTE-as-base and NOTE-as-quote cases
Test against Ambient DEX fork with actual parameters
Verify refund path is triggered when swap fails

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an attack per se, but a code defect causing total protocol dysfunction. Every cross-chain mint attempt fails at swap step, and users receive asdUSDC refunds instead of ASD tokens.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete protocol non-functionality; users receive degraded asdUSDC tokens instead of intended ASD

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No integration test verifying correct swap direction with Ambient DEX

validation_result.impact_summary: All cross-chain minting swaps fail due to inverted buy/sell direction, causing users to receive asdUSDC refunds instead of ASD tokens.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The `_swapOFTForNote` function is intended to swap `asdUSDC` for `NOTE` on the Ambient DEX (CrocSwap). In CrocSwap, the `isBuy` parameter determines the swap direction: `true` means buy the base token (paying quote), and `false` means sell the base token (receiving quote). 

When `NOTE` is the base token (`isNoteBase == true`), the router should buy the base token, so `isBuy` must be `true`. However, the code passes `!isNoteBase` (`false`), instructing the DEX to sell `NOTE`.

When `NOTE` is the quote token (`isNoteBase == false`), the router should sell the base token (`asdUSDC`), so `isBuy` must be `false`. The code passes `!isNoteBase` (`true`), instructing the DEX to buy `asdUSDC`.

In both scenarios, the swap direction is exactly inverted. The router attempts to pay `NOTE` to receive `asdUSDC`. Because of this, `calcImpact` simulates a swap where `NOTE` enters the pool (positive flow), which causes the slippage check (`-flow < minAmountInt`) to always evaluate to true and fail the swap. Consequently, every cross-chain minting transaction fails at the swap step, and users are refunded in `asdUSDC` instead of receiving their intended `ASD` tokens.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The evidence clearly shows `!isNoteBase` being used for both the `isBuy` and `inBaseQty` parameters in `calcImpact` and `swap`. While `!isNoteBase` is correct for `inBaseQty` (since the input amount is always `asdUSDC`), it is incorrect for `isBuy`, which should be `isNoteBase`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
bool isNoteBase = baseToken == noteAddress;
        (int128 baseFlow, int128 quoteFlow, ) = ICrocImpact(crocImpactAddress).calcImpact(baseToken, quoteToken, ambientPoolIdx, !isNoteBase, !isNoteBase, amountConverted, 0, 0);
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The fourth parameter to `calcImpact` is `isBuy`. Passing `!isNoteBase` inverts the intended swap direction, causing the simulation to sell NOTE instead of buying it.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
// swap is good to make, use call just in case revert occurs
        (bool successSwap, bytes memory data) = crocSwapAddress.call(abi.encodeWithSelector(ICrocSwapDex.swap.selector, baseToken, quoteToken, ambientPoolIdx, !isNoteBase, !isNoteBase, amountConverted, 0, 0, uintMinAmount, 0));
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The fourth parameter to `swap` is `isBuy`. Passing `!isNoteBase` executes the swap in the wrong direction, attempting to pay NOTE to receive asdUSDC.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When `lzCompose` calls `_swapOFTForNote`, the function determines `isNoteBase`. It then calls `calcImpact` with `isBuy = !isNoteBase`. Because the direction is inverted, the simulation calculates a positive flow for NOTE (entering the pool). The subsequent check `if (isNoteBase && -baseFlow < minAmountInt)` evaluates to true because `-baseFlow` is negative and `minAmountInt` is positive (or zero). This causes `_swapOFTForNote` to return `(0, false)`. `lzCompose` sees `successfulSwap == false` and triggers `_refundToken`, aborting the mint process.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an exploitable vulnerability for profit, but a critical logic defect that completely breaks the core functionality of the protocol. Every cross-chain minting attempt will fail and fall back to the refund path.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH. The primary purpose of the `ASDRouter` is to facilitate cross-chain minting of ASD tokens. This defect renders the entire pipeline non-functional, degrading user experience and leaving users with `asdUSDC` on Canto instead of the expected yield-bearing `ASD` tokens.
recommended_mitigations: Change the `isBuy` parameter in both `calcImpact` and `swap` calls to `isNoteBase` instead of `!isNoteBase`.
Ensure the parameters are passed as: `isNoteBase, !isNoteBase` for `isBuy` and `inBaseQty` respectively.
validation_result.replication_plan: Deploy ASDRouter, mock CrocImpact and CrocSwapDex. Call `_swapOFTForNote` (via a test harness or `lzCompose`) and observe that `calcImpact` receives `isBuy = false` when NOTE is base, and `isBuy = true` when NOTE is quote. Verify that the function returns `(0, false)`.
additional_testing_needed: Integration tests with a mainnet fork of Ambient DEX to verify correct swap execution and parameter mapping.
